title: Searching Inside Files with grep
intro: You know a function called fetchUserData exists somewhere in your project, but you have no idea which file it's in. The find command searches by filename, but grep searches inside files -- it reads the actual content and finds matching text across your entire codebase.

teach.title: grep = Ctrl+F for Your Entire Project
teach.body: You know Ctrl+F in your browser or text editor? It highlights every match of a word on the current page. **grep is like Ctrl+F, but for every file in your entire project at once.** Instead of opening each file and pressing Ctrl+F, grep opens them all, searches them all, and reports every match with the filename and line number.

teach.title: grep = A Metal Detector Scanning for Specific Metals
teach.body: A metal detector doesn't dig up the whole beach -- it sweeps across the sand and beeps only when it finds what you're looking for. You set it to find gold, and it ignores the bottle caps and nails. **grep sweeps through all your files and beeps (shows a result) only when it finds your exact search term**, skipping everything else.

teach.title: Using grep to Search File Contents
teach.body:
The basic pattern is: **`grep "text" file`**

- **`grep "TODO" app.js`** -- Find every line containing 'TODO' in app.js
- **`grep -r "fetchUser" src/`** -- Search recursively through all files in src/
- **`grep -rn "console.log" .`** -- Search all files, show line numbers (-n)
- **`grep -ri "error" logs/`** -- Case-insensitive search (-i)

The `-r` flag (recursive) is what makes grep search through folders. Without it, grep only searches the specific file you name.

teach.title: grep in Action
teach.code:
# Find all TODO comments in your project
grep -rn "TODO" src/
# src/app.js:14: // TODO: add error handling
# src/utils.js:7: // TODO: refactor this function

# Find where a function is defined
grep -rn "function fetchUserData" src/
# src/api/users.js:12: function fetchUserData(userId) {

# Find all console.log statements (to remove before production)
grep -rn "console.log" src/
# src/app.js:8:   console.log('debug:', data);
# src/utils.js:22:   console.log('result:', result);
teach.annotation: The -n flag adds line numbers so you know exactly where to look. The output format is filename:lineNumber: matchingLine.

teach.body: grep is the tool that makes you look like a wizard to clients. When they ask 'where does the app call the payment API?' you answer in 3 seconds instead of 30 minutes. That speed is the foundation of profitable vibecoding -- you solve problems fast and move on to the next paid task.

grep searches inside files for matching text. Use -r to search recursively through directories, -n to show line numbers, and -i for case-insensitive matches. It's your go-to tool for finding where functions are defined, where variables are used, and where TODOs are hiding.

steps.instruction: A function called 'calculateTotal' is used somewhere in the project but you don't know which file. Search for it recursively with line numbers.
steps.output:
src/utils/helpers.js:15: function calculateTotal(items) {
src/components/Dashboard.jsx:8:   const total = calculateTotal(cartItems);
steps.hint: Use grep -rn to search recursively with line numbers: grep -rn "searchTerm" directory/

steps.instruction: Find all TODO comments in the source code so we can track unfinished work.
steps.output:
src/app.js:23: // TODO: add error handling
src/api/users.js:9: // TODO: validate input
src/utils/helpers.js:31: // TODO: handle edge case

steps.instruction: Before deploying, find all console.log statements that need to be removed. Search case-insensitively in case someone wrote Console.log.
steps.output:
src/app.js:11:   console.log('User data:', userData);
src/api/users.js:18:   console.log('API response:', response);
steps.hint: Use -i flag for case-insensitive search: grep -rni "console.log" src/

steps.instruction: Find every file that imports from the 'api/' folder to understand which components depend on API calls.
steps.output:
src/app.js:2: import { getUser } from './api/users';
src/components/Dashboard.jsx:3: import { fetchProducts } from '../api/products';
steps.hint: Search for import statements that reference the api folder: grep -rn "from.*api/" src/

exercise.completionMessage: You've just power-searched an entire codebase! grep found function definitions, TODO comments, debug statements, and dependency imports -- all in seconds. This is one of the most-used tools in professional development.
